commit.message:
network: Exponential backoff cooldown for persistently unreachable peers

Previously, the reconnection cooldown was a flat 10 minutes regardless
of how many failure cycles a peer had gone through. This caused nodes
like 34.170.12.145 to be retried endlessly: fail 3 times, 10-min
cooldown, retry, fail again, 10-min cooldown, repeat forever.

Changes:
- adaptive_reconnection.rs: Scale cooldown exponentially per repeated
  failure cycle: base 600s * 2^(extra_cycles), capped at 24 hours.
  Example: 3 failures=10min, 5=40min, 7=2.7h, 9+=24h max.
- client.rs Phase3-MN: Add AI pre-check before mark_connecting+spawn
  for masternodes (same pattern already used for regular peers). Avoids
  spawning tasks that immediately exit due to cooldown, eliminating
  spurious log noise and unnecessary task churn.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace kCGImagePropertyICCProfile with CGColorSpaceCreateWithICCData

kCGImagePropertyICCProfile does not exist in Apple SDKs — caused
build failure on iOS Simulator (Xcode 16.4).

Replace property-dict injection with color space re-rendering:
CGColorSpaceCreateWithICCData() → CGBitmapContextCreate() →
CGImageDestinationAddImage(). ImageIO embeds the ICC profile
from the image's color space automatically.

Also fix pyflakes f-string warning in extract-icc-seeds.py:287.

commit.message:
fix: correctness, security & completeness review (#24)

* fix: correctness, security & completeness review

Phase 1 — Quick Fixes:
- fix(tests): update integration test extension ID and command name (Mistral → Ollama)
- fix(changelog): rewrite CHANGELOG.md with accurate Ollama history from v0.1.0
- fix(package.json): remove duplicate pullModelFromLibrary command registration
- fix(provider): add missing tool_call_id to tool result messages in toOllamaMessages()
- fix(provider): fix LanguageModelToolResultPart.content serialization (non-enumerable .value)
- fix(extension): make removeBuiltInOllamaFromChatLanguageModels cross-platform (Linux + Windows)
- fix(extension): remove unnecessary (vscode.Uri as any) cast; use vscode.Uri.file()
- fix(extension): handle LanguageModelToolCallPart in LM API fallback path (was silently dropped)

Phase 2 — @ollama Participant Tool Invocation Loop:
- feat(extension): implement agentic tool invocation loop in handleChatRequest direct path
  - collects tools from vscode.lm.tools, converts to Ollama tool format
  - non-streaming first pass to detect tool calls
  - invokes tools via vscode.lm.invokeTool() and appends results
  - loops until no more tool calls; streaming final pass
  - guards on toolInvocationToken presence and isCancellationRequested
  - falls back to streaming-only path when no tools are registered
  - renders streaming tool_calls chunks as markdown JSON blocks
- feat(extension): implement full tool call loop in LM API fallback path

Further Considerations:
- fix(client): align fetchModelCapabilities context-window detection with provider.ts
  (model_info.context_length → num_ctx fallback instead of parameter_size string heuristic)
- docs(provider): document why clearToolCallIdMappings() per-request is safe for
  multi-turn tool conversations (vsCodeId fallback keeps IDs self-consistent)

* fix(lint): address ESLint warnings

- Prefix unused parameter nativeToolCalling with _ in getAdvertisedToolCalling()
- Use non-null assertion (!) instead of unsafe optional chain on downloaded item
  in sidebar test where null would cause a TypeError anyway
- Suppress require-yield on abortedStream generator — converting to a plain async
  function would cause an immediately-rejected Promise to escape unhandled at mock
  setup time; the generator form defers the throw until iteration, which is the
  intended test behaviour

* chore: fix lint errors, format

* fix: Uri.joinPath, tool_call_id correlation, LM loop context buffering, CHANGELOG cleanup (#25)

* Initial plan

* fix: apply PR review feedback - Uri.joinPath, tool_call_id, LM loop buffering, CHANGELOG

commit.message:
Fix ARM64 register clobbering in buffer copy and SHA1

Separate byte_at() calls from store8() calls to avoid the Novus V0.1.3
ARM64 compiler bug where the second argument to a function call
clobbers the first argument's computed value in the register.

Affected functions: my_buf_copy, my_write_cstr, sha1_update,
sha1_process_block loop, sha1_concat, and network read loops.

* Fix searchBestPoint to use evaluatePointValue as tiebreaker instead of random selection

When the alpha-beta search returns the same value for multiple candidate moves
(e.g., all moves lead to a loss against an unstoppable threat), the agent now
breaks ties using the static evaluation (evaluatePointValue) instead of randomly
selecting among tied candidates. This ensures the AI prefers strategically
important moves (blocking opponent threats, completing patterns) even when the
search cannot differentiate between them.

commit.message:
Fix _byte_to_char, substr, substr_len for ARM64 register clobbering

_byte_to_char now uses a string literal lookup table instead of store8,
which caused memory corruption on Linux ARM64 when compiled with V0.1.3.

substr and substr_len now build strings byte-by-byte with _byte_to_char
when start > 0, avoiding copy_bytes_raw with non-zero offsets which
produced corrupted results due to register clobbering in the compiler.
When start == 0, copy_bytes_raw is still used (offset 0 is safe).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: ICC profile diversity in image generation pipeline

Add 4 new ICC variant generation functions:
- encodeImageWithICCProfile(): injects raw ICC data via kCGImagePropertyICCProfile
- encodeImageStrippingColorSpace(): re-renders through DeviceRGB (no ICC)
- encodeImageWithMismatchedProfile(): CMYK/Gray/Lab/truncated profiles on RGB
- saveFuzzedImageWithICCVariants(): orchestrates all variants per save

Wire ICC variants into saveFuzzedImage() for TIFF/PNG outputs:
- Each bitmap context now produces 2-4 additional ICC variants
- Real ICC profiles from FUZZ_ICC_DIR (round-robin)
- Mutated ICC profiles via existing mutateICCProfile()
- Stripped color space (no ICC metadata)
- Deliberately mismatched color space profiles

Add ICC variant encodings to encodeImageMultiFormat():
- tiff-no-icc.tiff / png-no-icc.png (stripped)
- tiff-icc-mismatch.tiff / png-icc-mismatch.png (mismatched)
- 7 named color space TIFFs (sRGB, AdobeRGB, P3, etc.)

This dramatically increases seed diversity for CFL fuzzers targeting
ICC profile parsing, especially icc_profile_fuzzer, icc_toxml_fuzzer,
and icc_tiff_fuzzer.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
